topic: A person finds out their biography was written without their knowledge
raw_output:
[story 1]

The crisp, cream-colored paper felt alien beneath my fingertips. It was a meticulously formatted document, a life story, and the words flowed with an intimacy that made my stomach clench. Every anecdote, every career milestone, every whispered vulnerability—it was all there, woven together by an author I didn't recognize. A cold knot tightened in my chest, radiating outward until my hands started to tremble, making the edges of the paper blur. I reread a passage about my early struggles, the description so painfully accurate it felt like an accusation. I crumpled the paper, the sound sharp in the quiet room, and stared at the discarded mess, the silence amplifying the hollow echo of betrayal.

[story 2]

I kept tracing the lines of the text, my gaze darting back and forth across the biographical sketch. Each sentence was a tiny shard of glass digging into my composure. A tightness gripped my jaw, and I bit down hard on my lip until a faint, metallic taste filled my mouth. *This isn't me.* The narrative painted a portrait of a person I barely recognized, complete with triumphs and failures that felt borrowed. My breath hitched, shallow and quick, as I felt a profound sense of being fundamentally misrepresented, a fraud exposed under harsh light. I pushed the document away with a sudden, jerky movement, the chair scraping loudly against the floor, desperate to escape the suffocating scrutiny of the words.

[story 3]

The revelation hit me not like a sudden shock, but like slow-moving ice water seeping into my bones. I sat frozen, the biography spread across the mahogany desk, the ink seeming to mock my very existence. My knuckles were white where I gripped the edge of the desk, the pressure building until my fingers ached. A low, involuntary sound escaped my throat—a weak, choked gasp. How could someone possess such intimate knowledge, such a precise map of my interior landscape, without my consent? My eyes scanned the text again, searching for a misplaced detail, a clue that explained this impossible intrusion, but the words remained polished, perfect, and utterly untrue to the messy reality I inhabited. I stood up abruptly, needing space, needing to feel the solid ground beneath my feet, anywhere but here.
